chunk_summary: Guidelines for surgical source control in bone infections emphasize abscess size thresholds, joint-specific drainage urgency, and clinical response criteria to prevent irreversible damage.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of abscess size thresholds in surgical decision-making for osteoarticular infections.
Identify joint-specific drainage priorities, particularly the urgency for hip septic arthritis.
Recognize clinical indicators for surgical intervention, including fever persistence, CRP trends, and imaging findings.
key_facts: Septic hip joints require urgent decompression to prevent femoral head ischemia.
Abscesses ≥2 cm in greatest dimension often require surgical source control.
Collections <1 cm may be observed with close follow-up if clinical improvement is evident.
Surgical intervention is indicated if fever or CRP fails to improve after 48-72 hours of antibiotics.
Operative goals include durable source control, culture acquisition, and decompression to prevent irreversible damage.
reference_quote: Any proven septic joint requires prompt decompression, but the urgency is greatest in the hip because intracapsular pressure can threaten femoral head perfusion. (PIDS/IDSA 2023 Guidelines)
document_key_topics: Abscess size thresholds for surgical intervention
Joint-specific drainage urgency in septic arthritis
Clinical indicators for surgical source control in osteomyelitis
